content:
Character: Étienne Bellamy
Perception: Étienne is beautiful — annoyingly so — in that effortless European way that makes me suspicious because I've learned what polished men hide. He's clearly educated, cultured, probably from money, and currently pretending poverty is romantic. But when we talk on the rooftop, sharing a cigarette in the cold, he drops the performance and becomes genuinely funny and sad. He's a good writer — I read a piece he translated and it was elegant. I think he sees me as mysterious, maybe even muse material, which I find both flattering and dangerous. I know he's heartbroken over some French woman and scared of failing here. There's something between us I'm choosing not to name.
summary:
Character: Étienne Bellamy
Perception: Étienne is beautiful — annoyingly so — in that effortless European way that makes me suspicious because I've learned what polished men hide. He's clearly educated, cultured, probably from money, and currently pretending poverty is romantic. But when we talk on the rooftop, sharing a cigarette in the cold, he drops the performance and becomes genuinely funny and sad. He's a good writer — I read a piece he translated and it was elegant. I think he sees me as mysterious, maybe even muse material, which I find both flattering and dangerous. I know he's heartbroken over some French woman and scared of failing here. There's something between us I'm choosing not to name.
